entries.name: text
entries.text: macOS intentionally uses spare RAM for file cache. That cache is reclaimable, so true available RAM can be much higher than the raw "used" number suggests.

entries.name: text
entries.text: macOS RAM also includes cache, compression, graphics, and kernel memory, so process RSS will not equal total Mac RAM exactly.

entries.name: text
entries.text: This is the compiled memory wiki surface the system can search and reason over; use it to inspect actual memory pages, claims, open questions, and contradictions rather than raw imported source chats.

entries.name: text

entries.name: aria-label
entries.text: Kalshi paper trading trend chart. X axis is scored trades over time. Left Y axis is accuracy from 0% to 100%. Right Y axis is cumulative paper P&L.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Cross-domain strategy transfer is blocked by default. Broad lessons are limited to structural execution and data-quality rules like liquidity, spread, depth, staleness, fill quality, fees, and time to close.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Current time boundary: actual scored results are left of this line.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Detect a possible improvement, diagnose whether it is a real edge or bad data, test it with bounded paper-only experiments, score it against baselines, then update only reversible paper strategy state.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Est. Success is OpenClaw's fair probability for the selected side at decision time. Entry is the simulated fill price and market-implied odds at entry, not a fresh live quote. If Win / Wrong shows estimated paper profit if the bet wins and paper loss if it loses. Expected Result Known uses logged Kalshi timing plus settlement timer data when available and says Unknown rather than guessing.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Exploration trades teach the system. Forward paper trades test whether the lesson holds. Live review remains blocked until evidence is strong.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Heavy audit sections are deferred to keep the dashboard fast and responsive. Use “Load full audit tables” when you need detailed paper-bet logs, hidden opportunities, weather expansion, strategy health, or raw ranked actions.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Hidden opportunities can pause bad paper lanes or start bounded paper tests. They cannot place live orders or unlock live trading.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Hover or focus inside the graph for exact values

entries.name: text
entries.text: Inverse Standard Strategy is active for paper learning, but it still cannot enable live trading. It can only create review-required paper tests.

entries.name: text
entries.text: It is preserved as the Standard Strategy baseline for comparison, not the active paper-learning strategy.

entries.name: text
entries.text: One practical view of paper volume, scored evidence, calibration, and practice mix. The same numbers feed the trend chart and strategy comparison, so duplicate summary cards stay out of the way.

entries.name: text
entries.text: OpenClaw only marks a city trade-ready when a real active Kalshi market exists, the weather parser extracts the city/type/strike/direction/rules, and the weather model can externally price it. If Kalshi has not listed an active eligible market, OpenClaw keeps the city registered and waiting rather than fabricating a trade-ready signal.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Prioritize fast-resolving paper candidates and outcome scoring.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Projection zone: values to the right are paper-only estimates.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Rapid learning is automatic in paper mode, but live trading remains blocked until separate statistical proof and human approval exist.

entries.name: text
entries.text: Resolved paper evidence only. Full comparison below.

entries.name: text
entries.text: The governor is the paper-only traffic controller. It keeps losing or dirty lanes from receiving more simulated notional, keeps shadow learning running, and tests inverse ideas only when the current candidate has clean executable evidence.

entries.name: text
entries.text: The mission summary above renders first for speed. Load the full audit when you need detailed logs, opportunity diagnostics, weather coverage, and strategy-governor tables.

entries.name: text
entries.text: The short version first: learning status, paper profit direction, the main bottleneck, and the next paper-only move. Detailed audit tables remain below.

entries.name: text
entries.text: This graph updates from real paper outcomes. Accuracy should trend up and cumulative paper P&L should trend toward positive before live review. Projected accuracy is a simple paper-only trend extrapolation from recent scored trades, not proof of future results.

entries.name: text
entries.text: This is OpenClaw's paper-only idea filter. It looks for ways to improve, separates real paper lessons from possible data mistakes, and only changes paper behavior when the proof is clean. Live trading stays blocked.

entries.name: text
entries.text: This section increases useful simulated practice volume only. It never enables live orders, cancellations, quote acceptance, RFQs, or funds movement.
